Phase-36.NAME: Resend Deploy Email Provider Wiring
Phase-36.Goal: Make clean production deployments configure Resend email delivery so verified-registration emails are sent instead of failing with provider_disabled.

OperatorIntent.item-1: The production email provider is Resend.
OperatorIntent.item-2: The installer asks for the Resend API key during clean VPS deployment.
OperatorIntent.item-3: The sender address is noreply@krotpn.xyz after the operator verifies krotpn.xyz in Resend.

NonGoals.item-1: No custom mail server, SMTP migration, or alternate provider selection in this phase.
NonGoals.item-2: No live email send in automated tests because API keys and real recipients are operator-controlled secrets.
NonGoals.item-3: No frontend redesign; user-facing behavior remains the existing check-email and verify-email flow.

DataFlow.flow-1: install.sh collects PUBLIC_DOMAIN, TLS paths, then prompts for RESEND_API_KEY with secret input and EMAIL_FROM defaulting to noreply@krotpn.xyz.
DataFlow.flow-2: install.sh validates that the Resend API key and sender email are non-empty and safe for temporary deploy config materialization.
DataFlow.flow-3: install.sh writes redaction-safe values into /tmp/krotpn_deploy.conf without printing the API key.
DataFlow.flow-4: deploy/deploy-on-server.sh writes EMAIL_PROVIDER=resend, RESEND_API_KEY, RESEND_API_URL, EMAIL_FROM, and EMAIL_VERIFICATION_URL_BASE into /opt/KrotPN/.env.
DataFlow.flow-5: M-040 ResendEmailProvider sends verification email through https://api.resend.com/emails; M-041 blocks registration activation until the email token is verified.

Steps.step-1: Extend install.sh input collection with secret Resend API key prompt and sender email prompt defaulting to noreply@krotpn.xyz.
Steps.step-2: Validate Resend API key and sender email format without accepting shell metacharacters, newlines, or empty values.
Steps.step-3: Pass RESEND_API_KEY, EMAIL_PROVIDER, EMAIL_FROM, and RESEND_API_URL through the temporary RU deploy config with redacted operator output.
Steps.step-4: Update deploy/deploy-on-server.sh .env generation so clean production deploys enable Resend instead of leaving EMAIL_PROVIDER disabled.
Steps.step-5: Add deterministic Resend request-shape, deploy env, installer prompt, redaction, and registration-provider smoke coverage.
Steps.step-6: Apply a live hotfix path for already-deployed RU servers by setting Resend env values and restarting backend after operator domain verification is ready.

Verification.command-3: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_email_delivery.py tests/test_verified_registration.py tests/test_email_verification_guard.py -v
Verification.command-4: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-36.xml docs/modules/M-040.xml docs/modules/M-041.xml docs/modules/M-012.xml docs/modules/M-001.xml docs/modules/M-048.xml docs/verification/V-M-001.xml docs/verification/V-M-012.xml docs/verification/V-M-040.xml docs/verification/V-M-041.xml docs/verification/V-M-048.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml && mygrace lint
Verification.command-5: operator-live: after krotpn.xyz is verified in Resend, submit one registration to a controlled mailbox and verify the email is delivered without logging API key or token.
Verification.command-6: operator-live-existing-ru: update /opt/KrotPN/.env with Resend values, restart backend, then repeat the controlled-mailbox registration smoke with redacted evidence.

VerificationMatrix.gate-1: Installer collects Resend API key through secret input, defaults EMAIL_FROM to noreply@krotpn.xyz, rejects blank or unsafe values, and does not print the key.
VerificationMatrix.gate-2: Deploy-generated .env enables EMAIL_PROVIDER=resend and backend Settings accepts the Resend configuration without exposing secret material.
VerificationMatrix.gate-3: Resend provider sends the expected POST request shape through a fake transport: endpoint, Bearer auth, sender, recipient, subject, HTML, and text payload.
VerificationMatrix.gate-4: /register creates only pending registration state, invokes email delivery once, and does not emit provider_disabled when Resend env values are present.
VerificationMatrix.gate-5: After Resend verifies krotpn.xyz, a controlled mailbox receives one verification email and backend/deploy logs redact API keys, tokens, and full verification links.

TelemetryRequirements.redaction-1: Telemetry may include provider name, masked recipient, HTTP status class, and safe error code.
TelemetryRequirements.redaction-2: Telemetry must never include RESEND_API_KEY, verification token, full verification URL, password, provider response body, or raw .env contents.

AcceptanceCriteria.item-1: Fresh install asks for Resend API key and email sender, defaulting sender to noreply@krotpn.xyz.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-2: Generated .env contains EMAIL_PROVIDER=resend, RESEND_API_KEY, RESEND_API_URL=https://api.resend.com/emails, EMAIL_FROM=noreply@krotpn.xyz, and EMAIL_VERIFICATION_URL_BASE=https://krotpn.xyz/verify-email.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-3: Registration no longer fails with "Email provider is disabled" when Resend API key is provided.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-4: Resend API key, verification tokens, full verification links, and provider error bodies never appear in logs, XML evidence, frontend output, or installer output.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-5: Missing Resend API key fails closed before claiming deploy success for verified-registration readiness.

Risks.risk-1: Resend can reject mail again if krotpn.xyz or noreply@krotpn.xyz sender verification drifts in the Resend dashboard.
Risks.risk-2: Free Resend limits can throttle registration bursts; rate-limit errors must map to safe user/operator messages.
Risks.risk-3: API key leakage would compromise outbound email; installer, deploy, logs, and verification output must redact it.
Risks.risk-4: Strict deploy validation can block clean VPS deployment if operator does not yet have the Resend API key; the error must be explicit.

AutonomousExecutionSafety.safe-local: Static source smoke, fake-provider tests, Resend request-shape tests with fake httpx transport, bash syntax checks, XML parse, and MyGRACE lint.
AutonomousExecutionSafety.requires-operator: Future Resend API key rotation, sender/domain reverification, live mailbox receipt, and any production Resend smoke.

ExecutionEvidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-17: install.sh now collects RESEND_API_KEY through secret input, validates EMAIL_FROM, and writes redacted Resend deploy config for RU.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-17: deploy/deploy-on-server.sh validates Resend env, writes EMAIL_PROVIDER=resend, RESEND_API_KEY, RESEND_API_URL, EMAIL_FROM, and EMAIL_VERIFICATION_URL_BASE into /opt/KrotPN/.env, and aborts before deploy when the key is missing.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-19: Clean VPS registration via email succeeded after Resend API key and noreply@krotpn.xyz sender wiring, closing the provider_disabled live blocker.
